text.title: Modelfiles, Docker, and Production Gotchas
text.summary: Baking system prompts into Modelfiles, Docker deployment with GPU passthrough, and critical concurrency and tool-use limitations.

rule.statement: Use Modelfiles to bake system prompts and parameters into a named model (ollama create task-agent -f Modelfile); this avoids re-sending system prompts on every call and sets context size explicitly.
rule.rationale: Default context window (num_ctx) is model-dependent and often too small for production. Sending large system prompts on every call wastes tokens. Modelfiles bake both into the model artifact.

example.code: # Modelfile for a code assistant FROM codellama:13b PARAMETER temperature 0.2 PARAMETER num_ctx 8192 SYSTEM """You are an expert Python programmer. Write clean, documented code with error handling."""
example.note: Build with: ollama create python-coder -f Modelfile

rule.statement: Ollama does not support parallel request processing by default — design agents to expect serialized local inference, not parallelized; never send concurrent requests expecting parallel execution.
rule.rationale: An agent pool sending concurrent requests will serialize them, not parallelize. Designing for parallelism leads to unexpected queueing and latency spikes in production.

rule.statement: Use /api/chat (not /api/generate) for tool use / function calling; only llama3.1 and mistral-nemo support it — check model compatibility before designing tool-calling agents.
rule.rationale: The /api/generate endpoint does not support tool use. Older Mistral models do not implement function calling. Tool-calling agents must verify model support at startup.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding the model name in agent code (e.g., always using "llama3.1:8b").
antipattern.reason: Different machines have different VRAM. A hardcoded model that exceeds available VRAM will fail or run on CPU. Model name must be configurable via environment or config file.

example.code: # docker-compose.yml for Ollama with GPU passthrough services: ollama: image: ollama/ollama ports: - "11434:11434" volumes: - ollama_data:/root/.ollama deploy: resources: reservations: devices: - driver: nvidia count: all capabilities: [gpu] volumes: ollama_data:
